data.title: India's IndiGo Orders 30 Additional A350-900 Airbus Aircraft
data.description:
By Cristina Gallardo 




  Airbus said IndiGo, India's largest airline, placed firm order for 30 additional A350-900 aircraft, as part of its plan to enter the long-haul international market. 



  These new 30 aircraft will expand IndiGo's total A350 orders to 60 planes, Airbus said Friday. 



  The two companies had signed a memorandum of understanding in June agreeing to this order, the aerospace group added. 





  Write to Cristina Gallardo at cristina.gallardo@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  October 17, 2025 11:31 ET (15:31 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Astromania Casino Announces International Launch Across Multiple Markets
data.description:
Astromania Casino Announces International Launch Across Multiple Markets

Astromania Casino Announces International Launch Across Multiple Markets

PR Newswire

MOSTA, Malta, Oct. 17, 2025

MOSTA, Malta, Oct. 17, 2025 /PRNewswire/ –The international iGaming market is growing with the opening of Astromania Casino. The new platform will be a complete online casino and sportsbook, with a regulated and technologically enhanced platform available to players across multiple regulated international markets.

In the early phases, Astromania will target multiple regulated markets as a multi-geo brand. The project demonstrates a strategic desire to enter competitive, highly regulated markets with a product that is supposed to be of the highest standards of transparency and operational stability.

Commitment to Transparency and Reliability

Astromania Casino provides an efficient and transparent platform that is developed on trust. It has more than 6000 games with international providers and a full sportsbook with all major sports.

"Astromania was created as a platform that prioritises performance, security, and fairness," stated the Chief Executive Officer of Astromania. "Our objective is to provide users with an efficient, regulated product supported by transparent processes and dependable technology."

Localisation and Payment Infrastructure

The platform adapts to each region's language, currency, and regulatory standards. Payment methods cover cards, e-wallets, bank transfers, and crypto, with regional options like GiroPay, PayID, and Paysafecard.

All financial operations are protected through SSL encryption, real-time monitoring, and AML/KYC verification in full compliance with European regulatory standards.

Responsible Gaming Framework

Astromania incorporates a comprehensive responsible gaming system designed to maintain player control and security. Users can configure individual deposit and time limits, apply self-exclusion measures, and access multilingual support around the clock. Behavioral analytics tools allow the platform to detect and respond to irregular gaming patterns, reinforcing player protection.

"Compliance and responsibility are integral to Astromania's operation," commented the Compliance Director. "Our mission is to ensure that entertainment is delivered within a framework of safety and accountability."

Technology and Expansion Strategy

Scalable cloud architecture is the technological basis of Astromania, which allows another cloud to run all of the performance without issues in the busiest periods. The modular design of the platform enables the quick adaptation to new regulatory settings and market-specific demands. The company is expanding across regulated regions, focusing on localized content and payment integrations for each market.

The company's roadmap includes the release of exclusive proprietary content, native mobile applications for iOS and Android featuring biometric authentication, and the expansion of coverage aligned with regional player demand.

Long-Term Market Vision

The brand's strategic goal is to combine large-scale content delivery with technological precision and full compliance with jurisdictional requirements.

"Astromania is not a short-term venture," added the CEO. "Our objective is to establish a platform that defines long-term reliability and transparency within the regulated iGaming sector."

View original content:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/astromania-casino-announces-international-launch-across-multiple-markets-302587742.html

SOURCE Astromania

data.title: /C O R R E C T I O N — Healthie/
data.description:
Healthie Reveals AI Scribe for Dietitians at FNCE 2025

Healthie Reveals AI Scribe for Dietitians at FNCE 2025

PR Newswire

NASHVILLE, Tenn., Oct. 16, 2025

The first product in the "Intelligence by Healthie" suite brings native AI charting directly into the EHR—reducing clinician burnout and administrative load across 25+ specialties, with optimized workflows for nutritional care and beyond.

NASHVILLE, Tenn., Oct. 16, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- Healthie, the leading infrastructure platform for modern healthcare delivery, today announced the public release of AI Scribe, the first product in its new Intelligence by Healthie suite. The launch was unveiled live at FNCE 2025, the largest nutrition conference in the U.S., with over 10,000 registered dietitians in attendance.

Built directly into Healthie's HIPAA-compliant EHR, AI Scribe automatically generates structured chart notes from telehealth sessions conducted via Healthie's direct integration with Zoom for Healthcare. Providers can review, edit, and approve notes before they're signed — eliminating manual copy-paste workflows and saving valuable time.

"Scribe by Healthie is the first step in our commitment to using AI to make healthcare more human," said Cavan Klinsky, CTO and Co-Founder of Healthie. "We're leveraging intelligence to abstract the clicks, reduce the mental load, and give time back to clinicians — time they can reinvest in their patients."

The Burden of Documentation on Healthcare Providers

Documentation remains one of the most significant pain points across healthcare. A 2022 JAMA Internal Medicine study found that more than 75% of physicians spend at least one hour each day completing notes after hours. Similarly, a 2024 study in the Journal of Human Nutrition & Dietetics reported that outpatient dietitians spend nearly half of each patient encounter on documentation and coordination.

"Providers are spending as much time writing about care as they are delivering it," said Stefanie Mendez, MS RD CDN, Director of Product Marketing at Healthie, who presented the new release at FNCE. "AI Scribe gives that time back, helping providers stay present with patients while ensuring documentation stays compliant, complete, and accurate."

Documentation fatigue contributes to burnout, lost revenue opportunities, and care quality challenges. Healthie's AI Scribe directly addresses this burden, helping clinicians reclaim time while improving accuracy and documentation quality.

AI Scribe by Healthie: A Native, Secure Solution

Unlike third-party scribing tools that sit outside of the EHR and rely on manual exports or middleware, Healthie's AI Scribe solution is built natively within the platform. The tool captures audio from a telehealth session through Healthie's direct Zoom integration, automatically generates a transcript, and produces a structured chart note within 10 to 15 seconds.

The note can be generated using an industry-standard format (like SOAP or ADIME), a specialty-specific template available within Healthie, or the provider's own custom-built charting note.

Providers can also associate different charting templates with different session types, ensuring Scribe adapts seamlessly to their unique workflows.

Key benefits include:

  • Seamless integration: Works with Healthie Calendar and Zoom for Healthcare.
  • Real-time transcription: Automatically captures and generates notes within seconds.
  • Customizable templates: Supports industry standard and custom chart note formats.
  • Full clinician control: Notes remain editable until reviewed and signed.
  • Enterprise-grade security: Built on Healthie's secure HIPAA, HITRUST, and ONC-certified infrastructure.

Healthie is a launch partner for Zoom for Healthcare and we chose to leverage Zoom's Real-Time Media Services to ensure high transcription accuracy, low latency, and full compliance across care environments. Providers retain full control — every note is editable and must be reviewed and approved before becoming part of the official clinical record.

Built for Clinicians, Powered by Intelligence

AI Scribe has been in beta testing for the past year, with early participants helping refine accuracy, workflow design, and specialty-specific use cases. AI Scribe and Instant Transcripts supports millions of appointment minutes of telehealth documentation across thousands of providers.

Early data shows that clinicians using Scribe save 15–20 minutes per session, translating into hours regained each week and a meaningful reduction in after-hours charting. The result: more time for patient care, reduced cognitive load, and greater overall satisfaction with the charting experience.

Because Scribe is built directly into the Healthie EHR, providers can stay within their existing workflows — charting in the same environment where they schedule appointments, bill, and communicate with clients. Notes are automatically generated within the appropriate charting template and linked to the corresponding appointment, ensuring consistency and compliance across teams.

By embedding intelligence into the EHR itself, Healthie enables clinicians and care organizations to maintain documentation quality and security while meaningfully reducing the time and effort required to complete it. This reflects Healthie's broader vision for Intelligence by Healthie — incorporating hundreds of workflows, tools, and automations that layer seamlessly into the clinical workflow, reduce cognitive and administrative burden, and elevate the quality of care delivery at scale.

Security and Privacy by Design

At Healthie, security, privacy, and compliance are foundational principles. Every AI-powered feature is built with the same rigor that defines Healthie's platform: HIPAA-, HITRUST-, and ONC-certified infrastructure, advanced encryption standards, and detailed audit logs that protect every interaction. As a company built for healthcare, Healthie approaches AI development with a deep sense of responsibility for patient safety and data integrity. All AI Scribe activity occurs within Healthie's secure environment, ensuring that protected health information never leaves the platform.

Availability and Pricing

AI Scribe is now available for 1:1 telehealth sessions conducted through Healthie's integrated Zoom for Healthcare connection. Plans begin at $35 per month (40 hours included), with flexible tiers available for larger practices and enterprise organizations.

Existing customers can enable Scribe within their account by navigating to Integrations → AI Scribe.

New users can explore the feature and watch a demo at www.gethealthie.com/AI-charting-tools.

About Healthie

Healthie provides the technology backbone for the next generation of healthcare delivery. Through its API-first EHR, scheduling, billing, and patient-engagement infrastructure and its expanding Healthie Harbor, Healthie powers thousands of organizations delivering millions of patient visits each month.

Reduce burnout. Elevate care. Healthie AI at work.
Learn more: www.gethealthie.com/AI-charting-tools

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/healthie-reveals-ai-scribe-for-dietitians-at-fnce-2025-302586466.html

SOURCE Healthie

data.title: Why Micron is pulling out of the data-center chip business in China
data.description:
By Britney Nguyen

The memory-chip maker reportedly hasn't recovered from a Chinese government ban on part of its chip business in 2023

Micron will reportedly no longer supply data-center chips to China.

Micron Technology Inc. is reportedly pulling out of the data-center semiconductor business in China, after having a difficult time recovering in that segment after a ban on its products by the Chinese government in 2023.

The company (MU) said, however, that it will continue selling to two Chinese customers that have a large data-center presence outside of the country, Reuters reported, citing unnamed people familiar with the situation. One of those customers is reportedly Lenovo Group Ltd. (LNVGY) (HK:992).

The memory-chip maker will also reportedly continue selling chips to customers in the automotive and mobile-phone industries. In 2024, sales to mainland China made up 12% of Micron's total revenue, or $3.4 billion, according to the report. Micron's revenue from mainland China and Hong Kong makes up about 10% of its total sales from the past 12 months, according to FactSet.

Lenovo did not immediately respond to MarketWatch's request for comment.

Shares of Micron were down more than 2.5% in recent morning trading, but had been down as much as 4.3% in premarket trading following the report. The company's stock has rallied 134.7% so far this year to be the PHLX Semiconductor Index's SOX biggest gainer over that time, as well as among the S&P 500 index's SPX top performers, due to high demand for Micron's memory components for artificial-intelligence chips.

In comparison, shares of AI-chip leader Nvidia Corp. (NVDA) are up 35.3% in 2025.

In May 2023, the Cyberspace Administration of China told local operators to stop buying products from Micron due to "serious network security risks" to the country's critical information infrastructure.

A Micron spokesperson said in a statement shared with MarketWatch that the company's data-center chip business was impacted by the Chinese government's ban and that it will continue to provide its memory products to customers, "while respecting and abiding by applicable laws and regulations everywhere we do business."

The chip maker has "a strong operating and customer presence in China, and China remains an important market for Micron and the semiconductor industry in general," the spokesperson said, adding that the company's assembly and test manufacturing site in Xi'an remains a part of its global presence.

Micron is one of the world's three leading memory-chip makers, alongside South Korea's SK Hynix Inc. (KR:000660) and Samsung Electronics Co. (KR:005930). The South Korean companies have benefited from Micron's ban in China, according to Reuters, as have local memory-chip makers that have government support. The publication found that the Chinese government's investment in data centers increased by nine times, to $3.4 billion, in 2024.

Fellow U.S.-based chip makers Nvidia and Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) have also come under scrutiny by the Chinese government amid broader trade tensions with the U.S. The Chinese government has encouraged local tech companies to use homegrown chips from companies such as Huawei.

See more: Nvidia is dealt an antitrust setback in China. What it means for the stock.

In September, China's State Administration for Market Regulation said it had found in a preliminary investigation that Nvidia had breached the country's antitrust rules when it acquired networking-technology company Mellanox in a deal that closed in April 2020.

The regulatory authority's announcement was referred to as "pure posturing" by Mizuho desk-based analyst Jordan Klein at the time. However, Klein said that investors should keep in mind that Nvidia's upside potential for revenue and earnings is still tied to the restart of its sales to China.

Read: Nvidia and AMD reportedly strike deals with Trump - but analysts see a 'slippery slope.'

-Britney Nguyen

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  10-17-25 1125ET


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Dow Jones Top Markets Headlines at 11 AM ET: Stocks Mixed; Trump Says Extra China Tariffs 'Not Sustainable' | Banks' ...
data.description:
Stocks Mixed; Trump Says Extra China Tariffs 'Not Sustainable'

U.S. stocks were mixed as investors weighed new signals about health of America's banking sector, and comments from President Trump about tariffs on China.

----

Banks' Strong Earnings Leave Investors Digging Deeper for Trouble Spots

Earnings from the country's biggest banks show a booming Wall Street and a solid consumer. But a warning from Jamie Dimon took center stage.

----

The BOE's Huw Pill called for the pace of cuts to be slowed in recognition of stubborn high inflation.

----

data.title: Carter’s, Inc. to Report Third Quarter Fiscal 2025 Results on Monday, October 27, 2025
data.description:
Carter’s, Inc. to Report Third Quarter Fiscal 2025 Results on Monday, October 27, 2025

Carter’s, Inc. (NYSE: CRI), North America’s largest and most-enduring apparel company exclusively for babies and young children, will report its third quarter fiscal 2025 results before the market opens on Monday, October 27, 2025.

The Company will host a conference call to discuss its results and provide an update on its business on this date at 8:30 a.m. Eastern Daylight Time. To listen to a live webcast and view the accompanying presentation materials, please visit ir.carters.com and select links for “News & Events” followed by “Events.”

To access the call by phone, please preregister via the following link to receive your dial-in number and unique passcode:

https://register-conf.media-server.com/register/BI3fde58f3c3f34528a1b4be7c2e5a567a

A webcast replay will be available shortly after the conclusion of the call at ir.carters.com.

About Carter’s, Inc.

Carter’s, Inc. is North America’s largest and most-enduring apparel company exclusively for babies and young children. The Company’s core brands are Carter’s and OshKosh B’gosh, iconic and among the sector’s most trusted names. These brands are sold through more than 1,000 Company-operated stores in the United States, Canada, and Mexico, and online at www.carters.com, www.oshkosh.com, www.cartersoshkosh.ca, and www.carters.com.mx. Carter’s also is the largest supplier of baby and young children’s apparel to North America’s biggest retailers. The Company’s Child of Mine brand is available exclusively at Walmart, its Just One You brand is available at Target, and its Simple Joys brand is available on Amazon.com. The Company’s emerging brands include Little Planet, crafted with organic fabrics and sustainable materials, Otter Avenue, a toddler-focused apparel brand, and Skip Hop, baby essentials from tubs to toys. Carter’s is headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia. Additional information may be found at www.carters.com.

Sean McHugh
Vice President & Treasurer
678-791-7615

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20251016397853/en/

data.title: Obook Shares Sink Following Public Debut
data.description:
By Connor Hart 




  Shares of Obook Holdings tumbled Friday, a day after they began trading on Nasdaq. 



  The blockchain-technology company, which operates as OwlTing Group, completed its direct listing Thursday, with shares opening at $68, or nearly seven times their reference price of $10. 



  The stock ended Thursday's session lower, closing at $55.55. It has continued to fall Friday, recently trading hands at $33.39 a share, having lost half of its value since its debut. 



  Chief Executive Darren Wang said the stock's debut represents an important milestone for the Taiwan-based company. Investor reaction, he added, underscores confidence in its goal to leverage blockchain technology and transform global payments through regulated stablecoin infrastructure. 





  Write to Connor Hart at connor.hart@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  October 17, 2025 11:03 ET (15:03 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: LuxCreo Receives Strategic Investment from Angelalign Technology to Co-Develop Next-Generation 3D Printing Materials for Clear Aligners
data.description:
LuxCreo Receives Strategic Investment from Angelalign Technology to Co-Develop Next-Generation 3D Printing Materials for Clear Aligners

LuxCreo, a global innovator in personalized medical and dental devices, announced that it has received a strategic investment from Angelalign Technology Inc., a leading global provider of clear aligner solutions. Through this investment, the two companies will establish a global partnership to co-develop next-generation high-performance 3D printing materials for clear aligner therapy. The collaboration aims to accelerate the innovation and clinical adoption of direct 3D printing technologies in orthodontics and to build a future-ready digital dentistry ecosystem.

This press release features multimedia. View the full release here: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20251017936321/en/

LuxCreo Receives Strategic Investment from Angelalign Technology to Co-Develop Next-Generation 3D Printing Materials for Clear Aligners.

Driving the Next Wave of Digital Orthodontics

As digitalization transforms the dental industry, direct 3D printing is increasingly recognized as an important future technological shift. It reduces manufacturing steps and manual processes, significantly shortening production cycles. With breakthroughs in new materials and further process enhancements, the precision, mechanical properties, and biocompatibility of 3D-printed aligners are expected to improve, further expanding possibilities for complex cases and personalized treatment.

Combining Expertise to Accelerate Innovation

Leveraging LuxCreo’s expertise in direct 3D printing and its proprietary ActiveMemory™ smart materials, the partnership will integrate the strengths of both companies across material development, treatment design, and clinical applications. The investment will primarily support new material R&D, intelligent platform iterations, and ecosystem expansion. Through accumulating robust evidence and large-scale clinical data, the collaboration aims to continuously enhance treatment efficiency and reliability, laying the groundwork for broader clinical implementation.

Revolutionizing Aligner Therapy Through Material Innovation and Direct 3D Printing

Building on LuxCreo’s breakthroughs in smart materials and direct 3D printing, the collaboration is redefining how aligners are designed and delivered for clinicians and patients alike.

  • Greater Precision and Control

    Innovative ActiveMemory™ materials provide continuous and consistent gentle forces, improving treatment predictability and helping shorten overall treatment time.
  • Enhanced Design Flexibility

    The technology enables variable thickness, zonal elasticity, and microstructure customization—allowing clinicians to create highly individualized treatment plans and address complex tooth movements with greater accuracy.
  • Improved Patient Experience

    By reducing reliance on traditional attachments, patients benefit from greater comfort, aesthetics, and convenience throughout their treatment journey.

Executive Remarks

Dr. Mike Yang, Co-founder and CEO of LuxCreo, added:

“We are thrilled to partner with an industry leader like Angelalign Technology. Their deep clinical experience and extensive industry resources will amplify LuxCreo’s strengths in material innovation, automated production, and AI-driven digital dental platforms. Together, we aim to establish a more efficient and precise digital orthodontic paradigm that benefits patients globally through innovation.”

About LuxCreo: LuxCreo is the leading platform for personalized medical and dental devices, dedicated to empowering doctors to transform patient care through innovation, speed, and customer success. We empower doctors to design, create, and deliver same-day treatments that enhance smiles, health, and well-being for a wider number of patients. Our unique ecosystem supports dental and medical professionals with onsite, scalable solutions and flexible production with laboratory design and production services with integrating the #1 3D digital dental printers, AI-empowered software and best-in-class materials. LuxCreo helps doctors grow their practices and improve patient care by delivering more customized and effective treatments with convenience and precision.

For more information, visit LuxCreo.com

Media Contact:
Email: marketing@luxcreo.com

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20251017936321/en/
